skills.name: dsa-random-problem
skills.description: Presents a random DSA problem from any category to practice daily
skills.instruction: Use this to get a random DSA problem for daily practice. The problem is selected from all 15 DSA categories.
examples: Give me a random DSA problem
I want a problem to solve today
Pick a DSA problem for me

skills.name: dsa-by-category
skills.description: Shows all problems from a specific DSA category
skills.instruction: Use this to get all problems within a specific algorithmic category. Available categories include: Array, Sliding Window, Binary Search, DFS-BFS, Tree Traversal, Dynamic Programming, Intervals, Two Pointers, Sum, Monotonic Stack, Word Break, Bit Manipulation, Trie, Combination, and Backtracking.
examples: Show me all sliding window problems
What problems are in the tree traversal category?

skills.name: dsa-by-difficulty
skills.description: Shows all DSA problems of a specific difficulty level
skills.instruction: Use this to filter problems by their difficulty level. Perfect for gradually increasing challenge as you progress through the course.
examples: Show me all easy DSA problems
Get me some medium difficulty problems
I want to practice hard problems today

parameters.name: difficulty
parameters.description: Difficulty level: easy, medium, or hard

skills.name: dsa-daily-challenge
skills.description: Provides today's deterministic DSA problem based on the date
skills.instruction: Use this to get today's specific daily challenge. The same day always returns the same problem, helping you build a consistent daily practice habit.
examples: Give me my daily DSA problem

skills.name: dsa-statistics
skills.description: Shows overview of all DSA problems and categories
skills.instruction: Use this to see statistics about the entire DSA problem repository, including total problems, categories, and difficulty breakdown.
examples: How many problems are in this repo?
Give me an overview of the DSA problems

skills.name: dsa-progression-path
skills.description: Shows the recommended order to learn DSA categories
skills.instruction: Use this to understand the optimal learning sequence. This path starts with fundamentals and gradually introduces more complex techniques.
examples: Show me the DSA progression path
What categories should I learn first?
